reviews.review_text: Just a game. Does what it says, says what it does. Works. Consistent. An RTS, nothing more, nothing less. Sadly this is rare now days, I always find my self coming back to this game.

I tried liking this game so much. I ignored the writing and worldbuilding that was apparently done by a nine year old, I turned off unit voices so I could ignore the cringy lines, I respeced the entire keyboard layout to fix the prehistoric keybindings - but the game's main flaw is and remains a childish understanding of difficulty.

It forces an iron man playstyle with no manual saves on missions that take hours, while making sure that letting even a single zombie through your defenses at any point in the game is an immediate game over. It announces waves that will assault your settlement at certain times, but then launches waves *between* those without warning or telling you. 

There is no feature to speed up time. No manual save. There's missions where zombies are just slow-walking at you for fifteen minutes, even though the result is clear after one. Every time you fail a mission, you have to start over. Most missions are full of lengthy stretches of utter boredom, fulled by such insane difficulty spikes that they force you right back into starting the entire thing from scratch.

Ultimately, this is the first game of a great genre, and will hopefully remain its worst example.
Game doesn't respect the player's time at all. It needs a save system that actually works instead of punishing the player

The game uses directional sound for the zombies, but it is always the wrong direction. 
I can't get the game to full screen properly without borderless windowed mode, and on a two screen monitor setup that means I can't pan right without using the arrow keys. 
The campaign uses the worst designed meta tech trees I have ever experienced. There is not nearly enough unit or building verity in the game to lock things behind campaign progression, so you end up having to slog through ten or so multi hour missions with only two troop(realistically one) and no building choices. The tech tree is also non refundable and too many of the options are mandatory if you want a chance of progressing the campaign so making the mistake of wasting tech point to try a new troop or building type, means you will have to restart the entire campaign.

This is one of my most favourite games. survival defensive tower builder with resource management. i love the lower bit graphics, the steam punk inspired theme, getting zombie kills in large groups is so satisfying. one dislike with a much love hate relationship is even 1 zombie in an unprotected area of my houses can end the game that quickly. its tense. i love it, i highly recommend it.
Good game if u dont mind wasting hours of ur time if u fail and fail u will if ur not a guidegamer.........

Parts of this game are genius. The core gameplay is absolutely difficult, forcing you to balance expansion risk with careful planning and detailed unit management. It's all Ironman, so you can't just savescum when you mess up. You have to have fallback plans and defenses with multiple layers. You have lots of choices in tech and strategy, all of which are interesting and all of which can work.

But for some reason they decided to include mandatory pixel hunt mini-game levels that have nothing to do with the core gameplay. It's like being forces to stop a tense game to play Where's Waldo, but on a poster sized image and Waldo is just 3 pixels tall. Just to do the campaign you'll literally spend 2-3 hours of your life scouring maps you've already cleared for hidden pixels. Again and again and again and again.

I barely recommend this game. 

From a gameplay, RTS standpoint it is pretty good. The survival mechanics and "turtling" gameplay are fun and feel like old school command and conquer. 

However, the game is brutally difficult, which I am fine with, but doesn't offer any way to load into a previous save state on any difficulty. Because of this, if you make one mistake you have to repeat about an hour or two of gameplay. This gets so repetitive on harder missions. Additionally, the player is expected to upgrade and spend technology points without knowing what they unlock, with some of the later unlocks logically being more at home towards the beginning and vice versa. 

The other issue I have are the swarm levels. They are simply not fun. Easily beaten by spamming soldiers and just waiting. There isn't really strategy since the zombies don't have any "lanes" and you don't get enough points to spend to create your own. You cannot speed up game time either, so even when you know you'll be fine you have to watch and wait for these zombies to shamble on over. The first 8000 swarm is just so brutally boring. 

However, the gameplay of exploring the map, defending against swarms, and the resource management is quite engaging to me. Therefore, I do feel like I can recommend it, but it will not be for everyone.
perfectly balanced game. if u are looking for a standard challange go easy difficulty. anything higher will sweat you. higest difficulty is only for the pro.
Fantastic RTS. Extremely punishing, but incredibly satisfying to finish!

Devs are too incompetent at making an RTS game; hitboxes are total ♥♥♥♥ and caused me to lose many runs just because instead of running, they hit boxes overlapping one another, causing missed clicks too often. I can't even keybind my own keys. Whoever made this choice as a design choice needs to never touch RTS development ever again; it legit makes me not want to touch this game ever again.

The world feels dangerous, the stakes always feel real, and every successful defense or hard-earned victory feels meaningful. It is clear that a lot of care went into the design, balance, art, music, and overall experience. Crazy good.

The game really doesn't respect your time (cliche I know) and has these random difficulty spikes that appear out of nowhere. Walls are useless and the concept of noise management in a city builder RTS that requires these high population cities is really backwards. Not only that, there is a chance through improper upgrade choices you could soft-lock yourself into a ♥♥♥♥ situation with the survival mission points. I really tried giving this game the old college try, but there are many design flaws that feel nearly impossible to navigate, or simply eat up way more time than other strategy games and RTS styles that do it better.

I am barely going to recommend this game. I will break it down like this:

Gameplay: For a RTS, it is fun. Resource management, exploring and defending your colony is fun and addicting. For some negatives, you cannot speed up time. Just pause. It can be a real drag. Failure is also frustrating, because you'll lose hours of progress. Some people like that, some don't. The game needs a save system. You also will be punished if you continuously lose. Like, so much so that you can't even progress further in the campaign. For a noob, play on accessible to start. Read the tech tree closely! 
The hero missions are also a grind, but both hero choices are fun to try. Swarm missions suck and that's all I'm gonna say.  

Story: I don't have much here. The story may as well not exist. Conquer land. Go here. Kill these zombies. Whatever. Voice acting is... mid. It's also confusing, but I respect the actors and I'm not going to disparage them. The audio is annoying at times. The unit promotion sound can be bothersome, but you can decide. 

Graphics are fine, the art style is beautiful. Very steampunk. Performance has been fine, nothing really to note, except one thing: it stuttered on a larger map with some huge hoards, but just once. I won't complain. Played on the listed specs, and another PC with a 3070 TI, I7 (don't remember exactly),16 gigs of RAM and did just as well. 

Overall, the gameplay loop is fun. The mechanics work.. somewhat. Research the tech tree if you're confused. You will be punished if you lose. It's basically a souls-like RTS. Give it a chance on sale, but it really does scratch that real-time strategy itch.

Scratched an itch I had for a RTS and I have always loved Zombie games. Been searching for something like this ever since AVP Extinction. So well done in my opinion. The ambiance, the cheesy character lines, the fear you feel even on easy mode of having to start all over from one mistake. Highly recommend if you are looking for a challenging RTS with great design. It is very simplistic but that's what gives it it's charm.
Spent hours on it! Reminds me of other RTS games like Starcraft and Age of Empires I used to play when I was younger!

A great classic-style RTS that I have a few quibbles with. The campaign has a good amount of mission variety, the style and humor of the game fits the setting (somewhere between Fallout and a Blizzard game) and the Workshop support means I can be playing maps forever if I want to. 

My complaints are two-fold: first, while the final game eased up on the difficulty a little bit, the standard base-building missions can still be very frustrating even at lower difficulty levels, particularly with the lack of save-scumming available (without using non-standard workarounds). I appreciate that it was a deliberate choice and some of it is just that I don't have the patience anymore to butt my head against difficult videogames (don't get old, kids) but having a few more options to tweak the difficulty (zombies spawned from destroyed towers, HP/damage levels) could've easily solved this and allowed old RTS-heads like me to enjoy the building and mowing down enemies part without having to do so much micro.

The other is the lack of content. I understand that Numantian is a relatively small team and you can't ask for TOO much from a $30 game but this game could've used a couple more mission types and, ideally, co-op. Gonna hope and pray for a sequel for some of that. 

We need more games in this genre with this level of polish and I sincerely hope a sequel (or another RTS game with similar mechanics) is in the works.
Amazing game, very stressful though. One mistake can doom the colony, it shows good how infections spread in a closed environment.

As you can see from the sheer amount of time I've spent playing this game in less than 5 years, I love it. Even though most of the criticism in the other reviews actually has a point. But in the end, it's simple: You have to get used to the game and its mechanics. Playing on higher difficulties is sometimes just insane. Even so, I've got pretty good throughout the years, and I definitely love the steam-punk art style and the general setting. Also, killing zombies is satisfying, specially with a Shocking Tower. 
Oh, and by the way: I'm actually a fan of the campaign that was added (for free!) to the game at some point. Takes about 75 hours if you take your time and play on 500+ % difficulty. Yes, the hero missions are sometimes tedious, but I generally like them.
Whoever likes tower defense games, plus resource management and (!) thoughtfully planning where to build your structures, this is for you.

An excellent zombie rts, nothing to jumpscare like a lot of them. Can be very hard. Nothing else is really quite like it. 
Agree that the hero missions can get a bit pixel-hunty. Would be nice if they were a bit more obvious.

One of the best strategy game i have played. Challenging especially at first, but super rewarding.
Definitely worth recommending.

I cant express enough how good of a hidden gem this game is. Its borderline edgy and goofy at the same time yes, but the dieselpunk/steampunk themes in the buildings and technology justifies itself is so damn beautiful, 

I just wished that the portraits of both zombies and units where not that... goofy, and the voice acting is kinda not tooo great... But nonetheless, the games is a general 8/10.

If you like strategy games give it a try, its going to fry your brain a few times

I've had a lot of fun finishing the campaign and doing survival runs, but frankly the cons outweigh the pros for me. I've done difficulties going from 25% to 600%. But I've also had multiple campaigns end permanently because the game corrupts the save and clears the entire tech tree, so your entire run is corrupted. This has started happening way more often lately, despite it being a fresh install.

The hero missions are just tedious timesinks. 
The swarm missions should never have been in the game. They are an exercise in micromanaging frustration. Set the difficulty down to 25% for them and save yourself a lot of frustration.

For the main missions, I've had times where a 2+ hour long mission would end in disaster because a single walker made it to a tent and started a chain reaction of death. But that's kind of what makes the game fun. Oh, did I mention there aren't savepoints, so if you fail, you restart the mission.
This game is great if you like a challenge with your city builders, i like rts and city builders but they never scratch the itch on both sides for me this game does

I don't agree with people giving the thumbs down because they don't like the campaign. I agree with them that the campaign is not great, but that's just one (small) part of the game. 99% of this game (and fun) for me has been just a map, a couple of buildings and BILLIONS of zombies. So big thumbs up from me.
my dad plays this game... he plays it.. a lot....... he says that it is good... but he wants multiplayer....

Very solid RTS game. The things people complain about in the reviews I like mostly. 
No saves during missions make the game more exciting.
Minigames require micro skills which I apprieciate.
Uneven difficulty among missions keep you on edge even if you easily finish a mission.
The techtree can really mess you up, which I like. Its fun to figure out which tech is great

Something I think could have been better is the sound. The voice acting is very bland among the units. Sound effects are also mediocre. The voice and movement of Quintus is very lame. I think maybe they were inspired by Immortan Joe but Quintus is a very sad looking emperor compared to Joe.
Also the balancing of units is a bit off. There is one unit (I wont spoil it) that is increadibly cost efficient/over powered. It would have been awesome if they designed the game so you had to go for a composition of units and every composition is good in different situation/maps. Now you have like a catch all unit which is a little boring.
Fine game, can't recomend. I kinda like it but ultimately it's a disappointment.

the only downside is that u have no save button  i mean if u spend 30 minutes for a mission. Sometimes  10 runing zombies come out of no where and ruin ur run. the game has better sound hearing but the buliding is kinda bad '' u cant have 3 walls in a row''.

A steep learning curve with real consequences? Sign me up! Your choices are yours to own, make do with what you can.

That said save backup is the only way to “save” your progress. Aside from that everything is a locked progression so check out the future research branch and choose accordingly.
an amazing stratergy game. up there with age of empires
Fun campaign not good, but still fun!

This game is stressful in the best possible way. You start with a few buildings, some workers, a little bit of hope, and the completely wrong belief that maybe this colony will be fine. Then one infected sneaks through a tiny gap in your defenses and suddenly your beautiful settlement becomes a very expensive buffet.

That’s what makes it so good. The whole game is about planning, expanding, managing resources, building walls, placing towers, and trying to stay calm while the map quietly prepares to ruin your evening. Every decision matters because greed gets punished fast. Expand too slowly and you fall behind. Expand too fast and congratulations, you just built a larger target.

I really like the mix of RTS, survival, colony building, and tower defense. The steampunk style gives it a cool identity, and the massive zombie waves are still impressive when they hit your walls like a bad life choice with legs.

It’s hard, tense, and sometimes absolutely unfair to your blood pressure, but that’s the fun. Few strategy games make survival feel this satisfying.
great game - challenging - hope they release another
Great game. Good Challenges. Be ready to get tested, it is NOT a walk in the park
If you're masochistic, this games for you. If you're not,  im sorry.
Fun Game, Art holds up fun to play,
Probably the best of its kind! Highly recommended!!!
fun until the harpys invade your housing area :(
They are indeed Billions. Tighten those defences!
Hard as hell, but really enjoyable.
Easily one of the best games I have ever played.
Good game bneed a second part!
Really good game !
Good game.
I mean, it's just dope. Tough, but dope
Best of the game for me
well made and fun
good game
Cool and neat
decent game
Best ever!
